name: Debug with Trace Viewer
description: Record and analyze test execution traces to debug failures with DOM snapshots, network logs, console output, and step-by-step action replay.
trendingReason: Trace viewer is Playwright's killer feature for debugging CI failures that cannot be reproduced locally
prerequisites: Failing or flaky tests to debug

steps.title: Enable tracing in config
steps.description: Configure Playwright to record traces automatically on test failures.
steps.tip: Use 'on-first-retry' in CI to capture traces only for failures, keeping artifacts small. Use 'on' during local debugging to always record.

steps.title: Run tests with tracing enabled
steps.description: Execute tests and generate trace files for failed tests.

steps.title: Open the trace viewer
steps.description: Analyze a recorded trace with the interactive viewer.
steps.tip: The trace viewer shows actions, DOM snapshots, network requests, console logs, and source code for each step. Use the timeline to scrub through the test execution.

steps.title: Record a trace programmatically
steps.description: Control trace recording within a specific test for targeted debugging.

steps.title: View traces in the online viewer
steps.description: Open traces in the browser-based viewer without installing anything.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Playwright Trace Debugging Workflow
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Trace Debugging ==="

# Check for existing trace files
TRACES=$(find test-results -name 'trace.zip' 2>/dev/null)

if [ -n "$TRACES" ]; then
  echo "Found trace files:"
  echo "$TRACES"
  echo ""

  # Open the first trace found
  FIRST_TRACE=$(echo "$TRACES" | head -1)
  echo "Opening: $FIRST_TRACE"
  npx playwright show-trace "$FIRST_TRACE"
else
  echo "No trace files found. Running tests with tracing enabled..."
  echo ""

  npx playwright test --trace on 2>&1 || true

  echo ""
  TRACES=$(find test-results -name 'trace.zip' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$TRACES" ]; then
    echo "Traces recorded:"
    echo "$TRACES"
    echo ""
    echo "View with: npx playwright show-trace <path>"
  else
    echo "All tests passed. No traces to debug."
  fi
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Trace Options ==="
echo "Always record: npx playwright test --trace on"
echo "On failure:    npx playwright test --trace on-first-retry"
echo "View trace:    npx playwright show-trace <trace.zip>"
echo "Online viewer: https://trace.playwright.dev"

faq.question: What information does a trace file contain?
faq.answer: A trace includes: action log with timing, DOM snapshots before and after each action, screenshots, network request/response details, console log messages, source code of the test, and video recording if enabled. All packed into a single .zip file.

faq.question: How large are trace files?
faq.answer: Trace files typically range from 2-20 MB depending on test length and page complexity. Use 'on-first-retry' instead of 'on' in CI to only record traces for failing tests and keep artifact sizes manageable.

faq.question: Can I share trace files with my team?
faq.answer: Yes. Upload the trace.zip to https://trace.playwright.dev for a browser-based viewer anyone can access. Alternatively, attach traces as CI artifacts and team members can open them locally with 'npx playwright show-trace'.
